profile.name: Chandrabal C
profile.bio: Innovative Embedded Systems Engineer (2025 Graduate) with hands-on experience in ESP32, ATmega328p, and IoT development. Proficient in Embedded C and hardware design using KiCad. Proven ability to develop real-time firmware and design custom PCBs for industrial and IoT applications.

responsibilities: Developing firmware for ESP32 and ATmega328p based smart applications.
Designing hardware schematics and 2-layer PCB layouts using KiCad.
Performing hardware-software integration, system test validations, and debugs using Multimeters and Logic Analyzers.

projects.title: Smart Water Tank Monitoring System
projects.description: Automated water level detection system utilizing ESP32 and ultrasonic nodes, featuring deep-sleep battery optimizations and ThingSpeak cloud telemetry logging.
projects.problem: Industrial and domestic water tanks suffer from manual inspection errors, leading to dry runs on pumps (causing mechanical damage) or overflows that waste water and power.
projects.solution: Engineered an ESP32-based node driving an HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor with optimized ping triggers. Formulated low-power deep sleep cycles to transmit level data only on threshold breaches or at hourly check-ins to save battery.
projects.hardware: ESP32 MCU, HC-SR04 Sensor, Buck Converter
projects.results: Achieved liquid level distance monitoring with less than 2mm margins; reduced average system power consumption by 65%; 100% automated pump motor cut-off control.

projects.title: Smart Vending Machine System
projects.description: Firmware architecture for automated product dispensing using PWM motor configurations, relays, LCD user feeds, and matrix keypad scan loops.
projects.problem: Traditional vending platforms operate using legacy microcontrollers lacking standard interfaces, leading to dispensing errors, slow user responses, and complex maintenance.
projects.solution: Designed robust firmware executing a keypad matrix scan loop, driving motor arrays via PWM relays, and providing character feedback on screen. Closed-loop inputs check if products are successfully dropped.
projects.software: Embedded C, Keypad Scanner, PWM Drivers
projects.results: Maintained zero failure rates in continuous dispensing test cycles; configured secure electrical isolation; attained responsive user interface speeds under 15ms.

projects.title: Smart Parking System
projects.description: Real-time parking slot management tracker utilizing IR presence detectors, optimized polling speed triggers, and a unified status terminal layout.
projects.problem: Drivers waste time and fuel hunting for open parking spots inside garages. Manual monitoring is slow and scaling standard loop sensors is expensive.
projects.solution: Built an array of low-power IR sensors configured to poll vehicle locations, optimizing sensor polling rates to save power, and designing an efficient serial communication bus to report open spots.
projects.hardware: ATmega328p / ESP32 MCU, IR Sensors, LEDs
projects.software: Embedded C, Polling Optimization, Low Power
projects.results: Real-time slot tracking with response latency under 100ms; reduced average vehicle stall search time; minimized standby power drain.
